message:
refactor(http/h1): redesign streaming body with channel-based architecture

Major refactoring of HTTP/1.1 body streaming to use mpsc channels for
backpressure and proper Cx integration.

New types:
- IncomingBodyWriter: Feeds bytes into IncomingBody with backpressure
- OutgoingBody: Consumer-facing body reader (yields frames)
- OutgoingBodySender: Sends body frames with backpressure + cancellation

Changes to IncomingBody:
- Now backed by mpsc channel instead of direct buffer
- Explicit Cx integration for cancellation
- Configurable buffer sizes and capacity

ChunkedReadState:
- Now Copy + Clone for cheaper state handling

Constants added:
- DEFAULT_MAX_BODY_SIZE (16MB)
- DEFAULT_MAX_TRAILERS_SIZE (16KB)
- DEFAULT_MAX_BUFFERED_BYTES (256KB)
- DEFAULT_BODY_CHANNEL_CAPACITY (8 frames)

codec.rs:
- Minor integration updates for new streaming types

This enables true streaming without full body buffering, respecting
memory limits and cancellation at every yield point.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
